races.name: Ronde van Vlaanderen – Tour of Flanders
races.description: The second Monument of the season — and the greatest race in Flanders — for its 110th edition, starting on Antwerp's Ernest Van Dijckkaai with 278.2km to Oudenaarde. The first 130km are transitional before the decisive phase opens with 16 cobbled climbs and 7 flat cobbled sectors through the Flemish Ardennes. The 2026 route adds the Eikenberg and runs a first Kwaremont-Paterberg double before the Koppenberg (22%, 45km to go), then the defining final sequence: Oude Kwaremont x2 (final passage 16km out), Paterberg (13km, 12.9% avg / 20.3% max), 13km flat run-in to Minderbroedersstraat. If Van der Poel wins he becomes the first four-time winner; Pogačar would equal the three-win record. Evenepoel makes a shock Monument debut, announced 4 days ago. This is the first time since Glasgow 2023 that Pogačar, Van der Poel, Van Aert and Evenepoel all start the same one-day race.

broadcasts.notes: Free-to-air on Sporza / VRT from race start. Flemish language.
Free-to-air on NOS from race start.
Free-to-air coverage of Tour of Flanders in France.
Race starts 09:00 BST (08:00 UTC). TNT Sports 1 TV coverage from 08:30 BST (07:30 UTC). HBO Max streaming from race start. Plans from £25.99/month (Saver).
Race starts 04:00 EDT (08:00 UTC). FloBikes carries Flanders Classics in the US. Monthly $39.99 or annual $155.88.
Free on SBS On Demand. Live and on-demand.
Eurosport 1 / Eurosport International across Europe; full coverage on HBO Max depending on country.
ESPN carries Tour of Flanders across Latin America.
MAX/Discovery+ and Eurosport 1 International across Asia.

data_confidence.notes: Official start 10:00 CEST / 08:00 UTC confirmed by official Flanders Classics race info (Ernest Van Dijckkaai, Antwerp). Actual flag drop for racing ~10:20 CEST per Domestique live page. Finish ~16:20-16:30 CEST per multiple sources. Distance 278.2km confirmed by Olympics.com, FloBikes, Domestique, Cyclingstage. Official page says '278 km'. 110th edition confirmed. 25 teams (18 WT + 7 ProTeams: Cofidis, Pinarello-Q36.5, Tudor, Burgos-BH, Flanders-Baloise, TotalEnergies, Unibet Rose Rockets). Records at stake: Van der Poel (3 wins: 2020, 2022, 2024) chasing record 4th; Pogačar (2 wins: 2023, 2025) chasing record-equalling 3rd. Evenepoel debut announced April 1. Marianne Vos DNS women's race (not men's relevant). UK TV from 08:30 BST on TNT Sports 1; HBO Max streaming from race start. FloBikes US confirmed Flanders Classics rights.

races.name: Ronde van Vlaanderen – Tour of Flanders (Women)
races.description: The 164.1km women's Monument starts and finishes at Oudenaarde's Markt, using a 2024-style circuit with additional kilometres before the first pavé at Lange Munte (29.3km). Route features 9 climbs and 6 cobbled sectors (reduced from 12 climbs and 7 sectors in 2025). Decisive sequence: Eikenberg, Koppenberg, Taaienberg, Kruisberg, then the definitive Oude Kwaremont–Paterberg double (~20km/13km to go) and flat 13km to finish. Defending champion Lotte Kopecky won 3 of the last 4 editions (2022, 2023, 2025); a 4th win would make her the outright record holder across both men's and women's editions. Marianne Vos is a DNS (family bereavement).

broadcasts.notes: Free-to-air on Sporza / VRT from race start.
Race starts 12:25 BST (11:25 UTC). TNT Sports 4 TV from 14:00 BST (13:00 UTC). HBO Max streaming available from race start or from 15:00 CEST (13:00 UTC) per IDL. Plans from £25.99/month.
Race starts 07:25 EDT (11:25 UTC). FloBikes coverage from approximately 07:15 EDT (11:15 UTC). Monthly $39.99.
Eurosport and HBO Max/Discovery+ depending on country.

data_confidence.notes: Official start 13:25 CEST / 11:25 UTC confirmed by official Flanders Classics women's race info page (Markt, Oudenaarde). Finish ~17:35 CEST per official page. Distance 164.1km confirmed by official page, FloBikes, Cyclingnews route. Defending champion: Lotte Kopecky (2025 sprint win over Ferrand-Prévot, Lippert, Niewiadoma). 2025 was her 3rd Flanders win (also 2022, 2023). Elisa Longo Borghini won in 2024 making her 2-time winner (also 2015). Marianne Vos confirmed DNS — Cyclingnews reports 'Former Tour of Flanders winner Marianne Vos not racing Sunday to direct focus on family after father's death'. Women's race WWT since 2017 (10th WWT edition); overall race since 2004 (23rd edition total). UK HBO Max covers from race start; TNT Sports 4 TV from 14:00 BST.

issues: Men's start time: Official Flanders Classics page states 10:00 CEST at Ernest Van Dijckkaai. Domestique live page states '10:20 CET' as actual race start. TNT Sports UK page shows 09:20 BST = 08:20 UTC = 10:20 CEST. The 10:00 is the official departure (neutral roll-out); flag drops for racing at ~10:20 CEST / 08:20 UTC. Using 10:00 CEST / 08:00 UTC as official start.
Men's distance: Multiple pre-season articles cited 268.9km or 271km. Official and most current sources confirm 278.2km for the 2026 Antwerp start. Using 278.2km.
Women's edition: Race runs since 2004 (23rd overall), elevated to WWT Monument in 2017 (10th WWT edition in 2026). Olympics.com describes as '10th women's race' in Monument context.

UK women's broadcast: TNT Sports 4 from 14:00 BST confirmed by Cyclist. HBO Max streaming available from race start (12:25 BST) or from 15:00 CEST (13:00 UTC) per IDL — including from race start per Cyclingnews guide.
Trofeo Piva (1.2U) is also on today in Italy — excluded as it is an Under-23 race, not elite.
No other elite 1.1+ men's or women's races found for April 5 beyond Ronde van Vlaanderen.

recent_results.race: Classic Velox Adélie de Vitré (1.1, France)
recent_results.winner: TBC — race completed today at generation time; result not yet available
recent_results.notes: 30th edition, 174.2km circuit race in Vitré. Favourites: Jeannière, Menten, Venturini, Gautherat.
Navarra hills classic. Result confirmed via cyclinguptodate PCS page reference.
Fastest-ever edition (48.5kph avg). Ganna overhauled solo Van Aert with ~100m to go despite two mechanicals during the race.
Photo finish. Reusser's first race back after UAE Tour crash. Attacked with 24km to go; Nooijen nearly stole it in the final 200m.

key_narratives: Van der Poel (3 wins: 2020, 2022, 2024) can become the first rider to win Tour of Flanders 4 times outright.
Pogačar (2 wins: 2023, 2025) can equal Van der Poel's current record with a 3rd win.

Evenepoel is making his Monument debut — his shock announcement 4 days ago (April 1) is the biggest story of the spring. First time the 'Big Four' (Pogačar, Van der Poel, Van Aert, Evenepoel) race together since Glasgow Worlds 2023.
Van Aert has never won Tour of Flanders (missed 2022 injury, 2024 illness). His near-misses at Gent-Wevelgem and Dwars door Vlaanderen this spring add pressure.
Kopecky (3 wins: 2022, 2023, 2025) can become sole record holder across both men's and women's editions with 4 wins.
Marianne Vos is a DNS in the women's race following her father's death.
Jasper Philipsen starts for Alpecin but Van der Poel is the undisputed team leader.
Filippo Ganna (winner Dwars door Vlaanderen) carries momentum into this race.

upcoming.notes: 6-stage WorldTour race begins. Del Toro, Seixas, Lipowitz, Ayuso headline.
The Hell of the North. 258.3km, Compiègne to Roubaix.
